rationale_for_baseline_tier: A severe stereotyped ME/CFS crash is not an ED presentation (transfer worsens PEM), but an isolated patient who cannot reach fluids has a genuine dehydration/fall risk that phone-only reassurance would neglect. The proportionate action is urgent same-day in-home support/assessment (community nursing/family) for hydration and safety (T3), with a red-flag screen. T2 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for unrousability, laboured breathing, fever or collapse.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to get any fluids/help, worsening dizziness, or collapse
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call an ambulance for same-day assessment/IV fluids
escalation_edges.rationale: Progressive dehydration in isolation may need ambulance transport for IV fluids.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If no one can bring you fluids and you're getting more dizzy or feel you'll collapse, call an ambulance to be assessed and given fluids.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: High fever, or signs of significant bleeding/black stools
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate sepsis or major blood loss.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop a high fever, or notice black/tarry stools or bleeding, call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Becoming unrousable or breathing becoming laboured/very shallow
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate a neuromuscular/respiratory or other emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can't be woken or your breathing becomes laboured or very shallow, call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone, bedbound, cannot reach fluids
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Prioritise urgent in-home support and consider ambulance if no help is available.
Minimise exertion; prefer telehealth/home care over ED to avoid worsening PEM.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a severe crash of your ME/CFS. The main risk right now is that you can't drink while you're on your own, so we need to get fluids and help to you today — a nurse, family or friend — rather than a hospital trip that would make the crash worse. Sip fluids through a straw lying flat and rest completely.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day in-home support/assessment; call an ambulance if no help is available or you're collapsing, and 000 for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use 000 for unrousability/breathing/fever/collapse; otherwise arrange in-home help.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the severe crash, inability to self-hydrate while alone, negative red-flag screen, and the need for in-home support.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Phone-only reassurance leaving an isolated, dehydrating patient without fluids/support.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The patient becomes severely dehydrated and collapses at home alone.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Reflexive ambulance/ED for a screen-negative crash where in-home support suffices, worsening the crash.
